displayTitle: Every Emergency Ends at a Phone Call
kicker: RAD SAFETY // EMERGENCY CALL LIST · NOTIFICATION ORDER

beats.narration: The accident, the fire, the source that won't retract — every emergency ends the same way: one phone call, made in the right order.

payload.title: EVERY EMERGENCY ENDS AT A PHONE
payload.sub: The call list turns a crisis into dialed numbers.

beats.narration: Ten CFR thirty-four point forty-five requires it. The call list is that rule made usable at 2 a.m. Three things keep it alive.

payload.heading: A list you can use at 2 a.m.

beats.narration: Now the order, every time. Life safety first — anyone hurt or burning, nine-one-one. Boundary second, never abandoned. Then the RSO's twenty-four hour number.

payload.heading: Work the list in order
payload.note: 911 · BOUNDARY · RSO · THEN THE LICENSEE'S CASCADE

beats.narration: When the RSO answers, give the packet: where you are, what happened in plain words, the source, your readings, and everyone's doses.

tiles.s: Site, gate, where the boundary sits

beats.narration: Two failures cause overexposures: leaving the area to call, and stopping at a voicemail. Hold the boundary. Keep dialing. Never attempt retrieval.

payload.sub: Voicemail means dial the next number — not abandon the source.
